turns.student: How have you been feeling overall lately?
turns.patient: I've been feeling pretty miserable lately. The abdominal pain and diarrhea have been constant and it's really affecting my daily life - I'm tired all the time, and I just feel like I'm not myself anymore.

turns.student: Tell me about your day-to-day — how has your health been in general?
turns.patient: To be honest, it's been a struggle to keep up with work and daily activities. The constant fatigue makes me feel weak, even after just getting out of bed, let alone going to my job as a software engineer.

turns.student: You mentioned some health issues. Can you walk me through what happens?
turns.patient: The pain started about 3 weeks ago, it's this crampy feeling in my lower abdomen that's mostly on the left side. It's like a sharp stabbing sensation that comes and goes, but it's always there to some degree.

turns.student: And when these episodes occur, what does your body do?
turns.patient: The pain is usually accompanied by bloody stools - it looks like bright red blood mixed in with my stool. It's really scary, and it feels like something's definitely not right inside me.

turns.student: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
turns.patient: The pain usually starts around 2-3 times a day, it's this crampy feeling that gets worse and worse until I have to go to the bathroom. When I do, it feels like my bowel is just not emptying properly, which can be really frustrating.

turns.student: During an episode, do you notice anything besides the main symptom?
turns.patient: Yeah... during an episode, I also get this feeling of urgency, like I have to go right away. It's hard to explain, but it's really uncomfortable.

turns.student: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
turns.patient: No, there aren't really any noticeable changes in my skin - no sweating, flushing, or anything like that.

turns.student: And your stomach — any queasiness or nausea during these moments?
turns.patient: I've been feeling pretty queasy before and after an episode. It's not always nauseating, but it's definitely a discomfort in my stomach that makes me feel off.

turns.student: What about other parts of your body? Any pain, tingling, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: Besides the abdominal pain and diarrhea, I've also been experiencing some joint pain - achy feeling in both knees and ankles, but no swelling or redness.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: I actually have had a low-grade fever a few times a week, usually around 37.8-38.0°C.

turns.student: Now let's zoom out. Any swelling you've noticed anywhere on your body?
turns.patient: No, no significant swelling that I've noticed.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your weight or appetite?
turns.patient: I've actually lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past 3 weeks, and my appetite has been affected too.

turns.student: Let me ask about your medical history. What conditions have you been treated for?
turns.patient: I had an appendectomy at age 16, which was a pretty straightforward surgery with no complications. Other than that, I don't really have any significant prior medical conditions to speak of.

turns.student: Any medications you're taking? Are you taking them consistently?
turns.patient: I've been trying to manage my symptoms with loperamide for diarrhea and ibuprofen for joint pain, but they haven't provided much relief.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle. How active are you?
turns.patient: I'm a pretty active guy, or at least I was before all this started happening. Lately, even just getting out of bed feels like a chore.

turns.student: And your diet — what does a typical day of eating look like?
turns.patient:
My diet has been pretty irregular lately due to long work hours and stress. I've mostly been surviving on takeout and fast food, which isn't doing me any favors. I try to cook at home when I can, but it's hard to keep up with a healthy routine when I'm feeling this way.

As for exercise, I used to be pretty active - I'd go to the gym 2-3 times a week and enjoy playing basketball with friends on the weekends. But lately, even just getting out of bed feels like a chore.

turns.student: How about alcohol or smoking?
turns.patient: I've actually been drinking more than usual lately - I'd say 2-3 beers most weeknights, but I'm trying to cut back due to stress from work and other things going on in my life. And no, I'm not a smoker.

turns.student: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
turns.patient: My sister's been dealing with a serious condition for years and has had to undergo some tough treatments. Other than that, I don't really have any close relatives who've had major health issues.

turns.student: We've covered a lot. Is there anything we haven't discussed that you think is relevant?
turns.patient: I've been thinking a lot about my recent work conference in Mexico and whether something I ate or was exposed to might be related to my symptoms.
